name: AI-WiFi-Recon-Tool
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``AI-WiFi-Recon-Tool`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: python WiFi_scan-v1.1.py  (then type $scan; serial port hardcoded to COM7 @115200 baud)

examples.command: python WiFi_scan-v1.1.py  (then type $scan; serial port hardcoded to COM7 @115200 baud)

algorithms: ESP8266 WIFI_STA active scan (2.4 GHz only) yielding SSID/RSSI/BSSID/channel/encryption
Serial aggregation of scan lines from microcontroller
LLM post-hoc narrative analysis of scan results via Ollama Mistral API (localhost:11434)
produces: SSID list with RSSI, BSSID (MAC), channel, encryption type (Open/Secured)
novelty: none — thin wrapper. The only non-baseline element is piping raw scan output into a local Mistral LLM for a text "analysis"; the actual recon is just the ESP8266's built-in WiFi.scanNetworks() API, which is strictly weaker than airodump-ng (no monitor mode, no packet capture, no PMKID/handshake/WPS/EAPOL). No novel recon algorithm.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
